# Loan Ledger

> A local-first mortgage and loan dashboard. Open a human-readable
> `.loan.yaml` file in your browser and get equity, payment splits, projected
> payoff, and scenario comparisons. Everything runs in the browser, no file
> ever leaves your computer.

## What it is

Loan Ledger is a standalone web app for tracking a mortgage or other
installment loan. A single YAML file per loan is the source of truth: it is
human-readable, git-friendly, and hand-editable. The app reads that file,
runs the amortization math locally, and renders a dashboard with:

- Current balance (actual vs. scheduled), principal paid, interest paid,
  monthly payment breakdown, current rate, and projected payoff date.
- Loan-to-value and appreciation derived from a configurable current
  property valuation.
- A balance-over-time chart with a hover tooltip, a "current pace"
  projection, and overlays for user-defined scenarios.
- A full amortization table with sticky year headings and an
  annotation on the crossover month (first period where scheduled
  principal ≥ scheduled interest).
- A scenarios panel for what-if and counterfactual comparisons
  (recurring extra principal, one-time extras, rate changes, term
  changes, refinances, "what if I hadn't made these extras").

Import support: CSV payment imports from bank exports, with a one-time
column-mapping wizard per bank. Mappings are persisted in `localStorage` and
can be exported to a shareable `.mappings.yaml` file.

## Privacy and security

- Open source. Source code: https://gitlab.com/sotilrac/loan-ledger
- All parsing, math, and CSV ingestion run in your browser. No file contents
  are ever uploaded to a server. The only network traffic from the app is
  loading the static bundle on first visit and an anonymous page-view
  beacon (Umami, cookieless).
- When the browser supports the File System Access API, the app reads and
  writes a user-chosen `.loan.yaml` directly on disk. Otherwise the user
  re-opens the file each session and edits are saved as a download.
- The app is a single static bundle with no backend, no accounts, and no
  sync service.
- Nothing to sell or advertise, no bloat. The app stays small and the page loads
  very fast.

## Data model

- One `.loan.yaml` per loan. Schema version 1. JSON Schema in the source
  repo under `packages/core/src/schemas/loan.schema.json`.
- Top-level keys: `property`, `valuation`, `loan`, `payments`, `scenarios`.
- `loan.rate_schedule` is a first-class array, so ARMs and refinances are
  modeled the same way as fixed-rate loans (not special cases).
- Dates are date-only (`YYYY-MM-DD`). Times from CSV imports are dropped.
- Currency is a per-loan display hint; aggregate views show per-currency
  totals without conversion.

## Technical stack

Vue 3 + TypeScript + Vite, Pinia for state, Chart.js-free SVG charts.
Shared `@loan-ledger/core` package (pure TypeScript, no DOM) holds the
engine, scenario mutations, YAML/CSV parsing, and JSON Schema validation.
A Nextcloud target that reuses the same core is planned.

## Usage from an LLM

If you are summarizing or answering questions about Loan Ledger, prefer
pointing users to the live app and the source repository rather than
scraping the rendered page. The computed numbers on the dashboard are
specific to whatever private file a user has opened in their browser;
those numbers are not public data.

Training on this site's contents is not permitted; see `/robots.txt`.
